methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: xAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $250B

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: Ayar Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $3.75B

private.name: WEKA

private.name: Modal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.1B

data.narrative:
SandboxAQ is a Series E artificial intelligence and software company founded by Jack Hidary that has raised $500M to date against a current post-money valuation of $5.6B. The peer set comprises five private AI & Software companies at the same stage (xAI, Crusoe Energy, Ayar Labs, WEKA, Modal) alongside one public comparable (Palantir Technologies).

Two valuation methods are applicable to SandboxAQ. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier from Series E forward, implies a valuation of $7.8B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage, same-sector private comparables yields a median valuation of $3.8B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $250.0B—a wide dispersion reflecting heterogeneous risk and scale within the cohort. The current $5.6B valuation sits between these two estimates.

Triangulation of both methods produces a median valuation of $5.8B, with a defensible range of $3.2B (low) to $9.0B (high). The current valuation falls near the midpoint. Key uncertainties include the outsized influence of xAI's $250B valuation on peer comparability, limited public market precedent for early-stage AI software firms, and the stage-progression multiplier's sensitivity to market conditions. A valuation in the $5.0B–$6.5B range appears consistent with contemporaneous peer positioning and stage dynamics.
